title: Maris Line in 20
wikitext:
[]: Mari's Line in 2.0             Quote Post by ImpedingAngel » Thu Jul 15, 2010 11:27 pm What do she mean when she said, "The unit isn't holding out, EITHER " when she's merkin' Tunniel in Acheron using Unit-05. Is she implying that this is not the first time has happened. Speculate.

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Thu Jul 15, 2010 11:45 pm Should absolutely be moved to the "Rebuild" section, but yeah, it would seem so. Or she could be talking about the angel.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Jul 16, 2010 12:55 am My mind is a little fuzzy about the context, but I'm fairly certain she's referring to the Angel.

[]:             Quote Post by ImpedingAngel » Fri Jul 16, 2010 2:28 am In the translation thread HERE . Mari is in the process of slaying Tunniel, when right before she notes her Robocop Arms start losing juice, she makes the statement in question and then gives Unit-05 "an arm". But the point of interest in this for me is, based on my speculations, that before she encountered the Angel, she said to the operators of the Bethany Base, "I'm finally getting to pilot, so that's okay" as a cover (as she is a spy of sorts) for some prior event involving piloting something else, hence dropping the line about having another unit give up on her. purely theoretical, but worth looking into.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Jul 16, 2010 2:59 am She just meant Eva05 and herself were too close to becoming FUBAR with how little time was left to finish the battle.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Fri Jul 16, 2010 3:29 am When she got out to the sea after Unit-05 self-destructed, she said something along the lines that piloting Eva is even more fun than people told her. To me, this implies that it's her first time. Note that at that point, nobody could have possibly picked up her words, so she was talking to herself. Also, unless an Eva was used in combat against anything other than an Angel, then she couldn't have participated in active Eva combat prior to Tunniel, unless she fought Lilith or Adam (highly improbable, and the other Angels are all accounted for). P.S.: If anyone posts "But she could have fought in a previous timeline/extra dimension/alternate universe, OMG!!11!1"... know that you just made a grown man cry. Seriously. I'm going to bury my face in my palms, and in all my two meters (seven feet and some inches for you Anglosaxons) tall glory, i'm going to sob like a little girl.

[]:             Quote Post by ImpedingAngel » Fri Jul 16, 2010 3:33 am Fears be squashed. *POOF*

[]:             Quote Post by CtHuLhU » Fri Jul 16, 2010 9:49 am 時間がない!機体も持たない! Out of time! The Unit's breaking down! (lit: The Unit also isn't holding out!)
